name: Human Rights Legal Support Centre
address: 400 University Avenue, 7th Floor, Toronto, Ontario M7A 1X8
description:
They don’t care about what you have to say. They offer no help, no insight and even decided that your case probably would be dismissed even before I heard back from the HRTO about discrimination in the hiring place. They were reluctant and disgusting in their behaviour and extremely disengaging. Do yourself a favour, don’t call these people and just file directly with the HRTO.

The call back is a scam here. I requested a call back and after an hour I called again and waited for over 2 hours and still haven't received the call back. It has been 2 hours now and I'm still on the line saying 2 caller ahead of me. Probably the staff waiting to closing time and leave. Now is 3 hours of wait time and no response. It states average call volume. Over 3 hours of wait is average for them. I'm saying 3 hours and this is while I'm still waiting on the line. The wait could be until tomorrow.

Wil is a really knowledgeable and helpful legal advisor. He provided excellent guidance for my case. I highly recommend the HRLSC. Thank you, Wil!

They're just a hotline at best - they don't really do anything to support your case by any stretch. They just leave you high and dry to fend for yourself against a tribunal that's already infamous for being a misnomer.

Very human centered, I worked with Rani, Oshish they are very competent and listening. I recommend this

name: AssessmentClinic.ca
address: Ontario-wide (virtual and in-person)
description:
Melissa was the Representative I spoke to regarding my inquiry. The service she provided was stellar.  She not only was very helpful and knowledgeable but also very pleasant to speak with.  More companies should invest in employees like this as it leaves a lasting impression

The staff here were helpful every step of the way. I was in contact with a lady named samatha she was kind and helped me understand the steps needed to not waste time and made my experience a fantastic one.

Ontario Independent Assessment Center is extremely professional in their communications with me. They are easily available and their follow up is also very good. They are very courteous and respectful in their interactions with me.  It is a highly pleasant experience dealing with Ontario Independent Assessment Center.

I had a very pleasant experience when speaking with the knowledgeable staff. Samantha was particularly kind and patient when helping me navigate any questions that I had.

My overall experience has been a very positive one.
Everyone that I have been in contact with has been very friendly, patient and helpful. I feel comfortable with everyone, especially dealing with my situation.
Thank you everyone.
Blessings,
Marcia

name: Canadian Paediatric Society
address: 100-2305 St. Laurent Blvd, Ottawa, ON K1G 4J8, Canada

name: Bayridge Counselling Centres - Mississauga Office
address: 53 Village Centre Place, Unit 208, Mississauga, ON L4Z 1V9
description:
This year I had my first ever experience with therapy and I couldn’t recommend Bayridge Counselling enough. I started meeting with Kevin-Joel Coupland following the death of my father at the beginning of the year and he has been a tremendous outlet of support during this time. Kevin is very understanding and can read a situation very well, providing wonderful insight and advice. I’ve been seeing Kevin for six months now and intend to continue doing so for the months to come. I would highly recommend Kevin and his services!

Kevin-Joel Coupland has been our counsellor for nearly two years now. He is incredibly patient and understanding, and genuinely cares about our lives. His appointment times are flexible, allowing us to work around our schedules. Kevin has an incredible ability for helping us work through anything we’re dealing with, whether it’s related to family, personal, or relationship issues. We are truly thankful that we found him.

I've been receiving therapy from Kevin-Joel Coupland for about a year, following a tragic loss in my family. While Kevin is very compassionate and empathetic, he also challenges my belief systems and gently encourages me to see beyond my circumstances to a world of possibilty. He shows his vulnerability through his own life experiences which makes it very easy to build trust and rapport. I would highly recommend Kevin as a therapist.

My wife and I have been seeing Kevin for couples' counselling for over two years and he has provided us with guidance during challenging times.  He is a kind, compassionate and knowledgeable therapist who treats us with respect and dignity.  We continue to turn to him for counsel and advice whenever needed.

My experience with Kevin-Joel Coupland was absolutely amazing. He is great at understanding multicultural contexts, and that helped me as I could relate to him as someone of my own cultural background.All along the therapeutic process, I became very skilful at communicating,understanding, handling my conflicts and recovering from my traumas. I wish him all the the best for his career.

name: West Humber Collegiate Institute
address: 1675 Martin Grove Rd, Etobicoke, ON M9V 3S3

name: Cognitive Therapy Centre - Cognitive & Interpersonal Therapy Centre
address: 20 Eglinton Ave. West Suite 1007, P.O. Box 2019, M4R 1K8
description:
CITC is an exceptionally welcoming and supportive therapy practice. The clinic therapists are effective, expert, and empathic, and Dr. McBride is very committed to the wellness of clients and therapists. CITC is a vital part of the North Toronto mental health community

Have had a very positive experience referring clients to CITC professionals. Positive feedback from my clients as well! Highly recommend!

I’d not recommend this clinic for CBT or any other treatment.

This is not my first time seeking a particular modality for a particular target. I had far better experiences and results seeking skill-based treatment at hospital and private psychologists who are strictly adherent to transparency in fit assessment, and treatment plan from day one.

Here there was very little clarity for 13-15 sessions till I sought my clinic chart for a second opinion. That’s when they told me I needed to see a psychologist with different skill set outside the clinic. If I hadn’t sought my chart, I’d lose more time. The CBT training I received elsewhere was far more clear within 3-4 sessions.

Highly qualified and compassionate therapists!

Great therapy centre and extremely professional.

name: De dwa da dehs nye's Aboriginal Health Centre
address: 678 Main Street East, Hamilton, ON L8M 1K2
description:
Great health centre. Very friendly and professional staff. Always looking out for their patients. Highly recommended

During covid I was denied access to medical treatment by All hospitals & 12 walk-in clinics in Hamilton And my Essential shopping by almost all grocery stores, even with a medical exemption letter because I can’t wear a mask!!  My NP helped me through the whole ordeal  when I needed stitches on my left forefinger to to see me in person but I was 3 days too late for stitches!!  - with Video conferences he could see what I needed & with advice I healed myself & have been since!! He also arranged video mental health counselling through St Joe’s programs!  For 15 yrs, they All have been very good to me & helping me get through any hurdles in my health & wellness!!

I ( and my family ) have been under the care of the doctors at the Aboriginal Health center for over 14 years. I have brain cancer and other health issues; I honestly wouldn't trust anyone else with my health issues. My doctor has always been friendly and professional.  The staff are some of the most caring people I have ever had the pleasure to meet. I highly recommend them! They go above and beyond.  My many thanks to my doctors and traditional elders there ❤

Hamilton Centre: Awesome Healthcare Staff. The Receptionists are helpful as well. To get the best care, know that the Dr's (except for one) are only temporary, and you will be transferred to different people. However, they have your Info and data. Make a list of your healthcare concern questions for during your visit. They also offer Community Programs, so they take care of both the physical and mental healh aspects of our being. Five stars all the way.

I absolutely adore the health care staff. They genuinely care about my well being. I have been a patient here since I was a baby. 10/10 amazing people over all!

name: De dwa da dehs nye's Aboriginal Health Centre - Brantford Site
address: 36 King Street, Brantford, ON N3T 3C5
description:
Everyone who works at the brantford office is amazing! Very caring and very detailed about your health and any concerns you have!

When they say they will “prioritize” your file, what they mean to say is that they will put your file in the “I’ll deal with this later” pile and then call you almost 2 weeks later to tell you that you need to do more before they can consider caring to even look to see that your situation might actually be serious.

There is no actual support here. It’s barely politically correct.
Creator will help you more than they will, guaranteed.

The aborigianl health clinic is the worst place to go for health care. If you have been healthy for a 3 years and dont need a doctor for that long then they kick you out of the system and you no longer have health care, They barely have a doctor and when they do have one its some grumpy old 90 year old man that should be retired. When their practical nurses wait on you they pull up the wrong file and ask how your cancer is which is alarming and hurtful until you realize their looking at the wrong chart. I wonder how many people have died because of their incompetence. They should be closed down.

AHC is a great place for me, since I’m diabetic they have everything there like a foot doctor, nutritionist, diabetic health nurse, blood work etc, and they are very nice to. : )

I have had nothing but good experiences when having to deal with this Dr's office. The staff are doing an excellent job.

Received quality care

This is such an amazing organization. With so many different programs and practics. I am apart of many things they have to offer. The great thing is they have such a wide range of programs there is something for everyone and they take pride in the work they do.

Staff went over and above to address my needs. They were professional and diligent in manner with issues I brought forward to them.
Miigwetch for such a great experience.

No one answers calls or texts...

name: Southwest Ontario Aboriginal Health Access Centre - Chippewas of the Thames Site
address: 77 Anishinaabeg Drive, Muncey, ON N0L 1Y0
description:
The centre has a real Community feel to it. It is kept very clean and covid standards are kept at a high level of standard. There is multiple services within the centre and there are public restrooms there as well. A pharmacy under Pharmasave is also within the centre which saves a person on Transportation costs to get their medications

Position 1 in queue 20 minutes, no receptionist to answer, annoying music, general answering machine message is too long. SOAHAC

Great health care professionals and admin staff are nice and kind,

Expansion of services & the staff is friendly!

name: Southwest Ontario Aboriginal Health Access Centre - Windsor Site
address: 2-1405 Tecumseh Rd W., Windsor, ON N9B 1T7
description:
After messing up my appointments multiple times and just unwilling to help (needed a specialist which they didnt want to send me to needed a dr. Note to see the specialist) i decided to go to a regular clinic and got the help right away !

SOHAC is where I’ve chosen to have my “wholistic” healing needs met.
It is where I am treated with patience and dignity; with time enough to address and understand my issues; where I am heard and listened to; where I see a wonderful team who interact well to and for my benefit, and where a wide variety of healing methods are employed.
They consider me when special events are taking place and call me immediately when tests results are returned.
SOHAC staff interact well with me and with each other.

When i call the voicemail says it's closed but it's only 3:15pm. The office hours say closed at 4:30 pm? The voicemail doesn't let me go further. Frustrating.

Windsor SOAHAC centre is a joke... mess up your appointments... they do not treat very many people with dignity or respect... if you call to lodge a complaint they dont help...very poorly run facility... get a REAL doctor or go to a  REAL CLINIC

Good mix of Indigenous and Western medicine with lots of programming opportunities.

name: Temiskaming Native Women’s Support Group
address: 121 Scott Street, New Liskeard, ON P0J 1P0
description: Amazing health team who has treated my family with nothing but respect and dignity. They have a great staff and Dawn the receptionist is awesome and well involved in helping members and putting smiles on their faces. Smiles are contagious!

name: Wabano Centre for Aboriginal Health
address: 299 Montreal Road, Ottawa, ON K1L 6B8
description:
Ottawa Canada here and I've literally traveled all over the world a few times and I've been to a lot of native centers and this place is the absolute best ❤️🙏the staff is great their programs are fabulous they have fed me when I was hungry
They put clothes on my back when I had nothing and they put a roof over my head when I had no roof on my head.
I can never tell you how grateful I will always be for these wonderful people who work there at Wabbano

We rented the unit through one of Wabano’s housing initiatives as they promised continuous support for this person after they moved in. Unfortunately, they ceased to provide any additional support and left the tenant and our property at risk.

Was just informed that prescription refills take up to 7-10 days to be renewed because two of their nurses left and they’re currently only open with one doctor. No emails, letters or communications were sent out to clients to let them know of the situation and now my young daughter who needs her medication might not get in it time. Their excuse was “there’s posters all over the office you would’ve seen them”.

This is an honest, generous review of my experience here. Their doctors and nurse practitioners are professional and compassionate - busy, but will do their best to help.

The mental wellness program is a good gesture, and Wabano does well at crisis intervention. However, during my time working with a mental health counsellor, crises would arise and I would show up to my appointments with no one to see me. I would consistently arrive with no one knowing I had an appointment, or my provider had been double booked. They were likely being overworked and under-resourced, so I do not blame them - they were actually great when we did have sessions.

Being ghosted by a therapist is still not a good look though, so it loses major points there. Otherwise, it is a great space, has a lot of good people working in the front-facing, public positions and I would look into accessing their services, but mindful that things get busy there.

First off, I just wanted to make a disclaimer and say that I love my doctor. This review is not at all indicative of the level of care I receive from Dr. Fidele Bana - in fact if there’s one positive I can impart on anyone who reads this review, it’s that Dr. Bana is an incredible healthcare worker. He’s attentive no matter how many patients he has to see in a day, he’s patient and diligent, and he treats you with dignity and respect.

My issue lies with the receptionists. Considering the insane workload that those in the healthcare industry take on, I’m not expecting a bright, sunshine-y attitude and I don’t fault the women at the front desk for being a little curt or impersonal at times (although in my opinion doctors and NPs deal with more complex issues and yet they’re able to maintain a respectful dialogue, so take that as you will) but it’s the lack of accountability that I fault them for.

Twice now when my pharmacy has needed to send in a refill request for my bipolar medication I’ve been given the run around and to add insult to injury, have been made to feel like I was personally responsible for their screwups. Their recommendation is that you give them at least a week to respond, yet the first time I encountered friction they had TWO ENTIRE WEEKS and multiple phone calls from both me and my partner (who had to step in on my behalf because of how frustrated I was becoming) as well as my pharmacy urging them to please respond to the request. At no point during this wild goose chase was there any contrition on their behalf, and considering the medication I need is incredibly important and comes with some nasty side effects, this is unacceptable.

I’d informed Dr. Bana about this experience and he was very apologetic even though it wasn’t his fault, and the next time he faxed over my prescription he had me stay in the office while he did so in order to make sure it went through to my pharmacy. He informed me that if I didn’t get a call from them in the next couple of hours to confirm that they received my prescription, to please call him back. Luckily that wasn’t necessary but the level of care and concern from my doctor should be highlighted as it’s a shame it isn’t also reflected in the staff upfront.

I know they’ve had complaints before about their so-called “attitudes” but as I stated earlier I don’t fault front-facing employees for not being “on” all the time as they’re human beings who certainly have to deal with quite a lot, and I imagine patients are not always kind to them. My compassion ends is when they don’t follow through, have the audacity to suggest the patient is at fault for their screw-ups, and then offer little in recourse even if just to apologize.

The lack of accountability is quite frankly insulting and worse still is that they’ll even lie to avoid it:

1. They’ll tell you that faxes typically take 24 to 48 hours to be received despite this no longer being the 1980s. Faxes take at most 15 minutes and even then that’s for huge multi-page contracts.

2. They’ll tell you that your doctor was away from office for a couple of weeks only for your doctor to inform you that no, he was absolutely in the office during that time and as such was more than able to sign off on the request.

And my favourite line of bullshit:
3. They’ll tell you that they’ll send back the request that day only for your pharmacy to receive not a single fax from them.

I think the most troubling aspect of this all is that we go to Wabano because of the way indigenous people are routinely disregarded, dehumanized, and degraded by the healthcare system, so you’d think that this clinic would be a relative safe space by comparison. But sadly that isn’t the case, and in some ways it feels worse being treated this way as it’s done by your own people. As the saying goes: Not all skin folk is kinfolk.

In short, the one star is entirely because of Dr. Bana and if we were basing the rating solely on him it’d be five stars. If he ever opens his own practice I’ll be the first to leave Wabano in the dust.

name: Waasegiizhig Nanaandawe’iyewigamig
address: 212 4th Ave. South, Kenora, ON P9N 1Y9
description:
I extend my deepest gratitude to Waasegiizhig Nanaandawe'iyewigamig for the invaluable support and guidance provided during my journey towards emotional well-being. Specifically, I wish to express my sincere appreciation to Rochelle Shibish for her exceptional counseling services.

Rochelle's expertise, compassion, and unwavering support were instrumental in helping me navigate through a challenging period marked by depression. Her personalized approach and insightful interventions empowered me with the tools and resilience needed to overcome obstacles and embrace a brighter future.

The compassionate care and professionalism exhibited by Rochelle has left a profound impact. I am deeply thankful for her dedication to fostering healing and growth within the community.

Since then, I will be wholeheartedly recommending Waasegiizhig Nanaandawe'iyewigamig to anyone seeking support on their journey towards emotional well-being. Your commitment to excellence and genuine concern for the welfare of your clients are truly commendable, and I am immensely grateful for the positive difference it has made in my life.
Gitchi-Miigwech

name: Alliance for Healthier Communities
address: 500-970 Lawrence Ave W, Toronto, ON M6A 3B6
description: Caring people with a great attitude

name: Ottawa-Carleton
address: 160 Percy Street, Ottawa, ON K1R 6E5
description:
The McNabb Recreation Centre is a versatile downtown Ottawa facility with an arena, gymnasium, dance studio, pottery studio, and multipurpose rooms. It’s right beside McNabb Park, which adds outdoor amenities like a playground, wading pool, skateboard park, and seasonal outdoor rink. Families and community groups appreciate the wide range of programs, from skating and hockey to arts and fitness. On the downside, the building is older and can feel dated in spots, and popular programs fill up quickly, so advance registration is often necessary. Parking is limited, though transit access is good.

I really like this facility, its location, and seeing a wide variety of cultures in activities participating together. Ample parking and city staff here just seem friendlier. Can host events can be a hub of of activity and when the weather is warm there is always a multitude of people hanging out after their hobby or recreational activity is over. The only issue I have is the cleanliness of the washrooms. Truly, the rest of the facility is impeccable but the washrooms often have strong orders and are very unkept. Doesn’t deter me from going back. Always look for city activities to do at this location.

Love this park. Full of nice people/dogs and lots of space for dogs to run and enjoy themselves in a fenced-in safe environment.

Most grateful the ramp to get up to the door was cleared well. Thankful the person who worked there was at the front. Sometimes they're not. Not sure how accessible the washrooms are.

My grandson loved it. The pool is a great activity for the small kids. Lots of playground fixtures, plenty of field for sports. They could really use a Porta potty as no washrooms are available.

name: Métis Nation of Ontario
address: Suite 1100 – 66 Slater Street, Ottawa, ON K1P 5H1
description:
Completely incompetent to the extreme and should be reviewed by the national government. My application was rejected twice when my mother, aunt and sister are all registered Métis citizens. They decision on who is Métis is clearly arbitrary and not based on proving lineage at all.

The people at the front office were extremely rude to us and were very short tempered. Also the ladies at the front office were calling us "rude" to one another in French, assuming we couldn't understand what they were saying.

Nice friendly people there

name: Leamington Campus Employment Assessment Centre
address: 215 Talbot St. E., Leamington, ON N8H 3X5

name: Ontario Colleges (ontariocolleges.ca)
address: 60 Corporate Court, Guelph, ON, N1G 5J3
description:
The non-refundable $110 fee is incredibly predatory in how they enforce it. I only applied for one program (a post-graduate 1 year program, not sure why I even should have to spend an entire application fee on that in the first place) and the program was suspended, so I have no use for the application and want my money back. The person I chatted with online was no help at all and did not care about the issue I was bringing up and just repeatedly would say due to the policy he can't refund me. Considering the ONLY program I applied to was SUSPENDED, I should get my money back because I'm not receiving what I paid for. They didn't have to send transcripts or do anything on their part either so it's just $110 to tell my college I already attend I want to apply for a program, and to get an email back saying it's suspended. $110 down the drain.

They not want to help and if you ask some more questions they not like and rude female Customer service representative. They just want money but not want to provide information. Waste of money rather than apply directly to college. Worse process. Zero stars.

Ontario colleges are scam artists. I have an official transcript that I recieved when I graduated high school in PERFECT condition but they refuse to accept it because it's declared as "unofficial". It's only unofficial because my high school didn't mail it in. What's the difference if I send it in then my high school?? Except for address and envelope there's nothing.  They just wants​ you to pay for them to retrieve it themselves.

The office is listed to be open until 5 pm, but I called at 4:45 pm and got an automated voice message saying that their office is now closed.
Unbelievable!  Are these unionized employees??  If so, no surprise.

A terrible site to have an account!! Because I haven’t used the website in a few years to apply to another college, they erased my whole account, records including my email address with log-in and pass-word as well!

name: WoodGreen
address: 1533 Victoria Park Ave.
description:
I cannot say enough good things about WoodGreen Community Services at 1533 Victoria Park Ave, Scarborough. This organization truly makes a difference in people’s lives through their compassion, professionalism, and dedication to the community.
From the moment I connected with them, I felt welcomed, respected, and supported. The staff are knowledgeable and genuinely care about helping people improve their lives. They take the time to listen, understand your situation, and provide the right guidance and resources.
I especially want to recognize Eden Abraham, who has helped me consistently and with so much kindness and patience. Eden goes above and beyond every single time. She is professional, understanding, and always ready to help. No matter how busy things are, she makes sure I feel heard and supported. Her dedication and positive attitude truly make a difference.
WoodGreen is not just a social service organization — it is a place where people are treated with dignity and respect. I am very grateful for everything they have done for me.
Highly recommended to anyone in need of support. Thank you, WoodGreen, and thank you, Eden Abraham, for your amazing service!

Professional and supportive staff. They offer helpful community services and guidance. My experience was positive and organized.

Great staff, they were all friendly, helpful, and you feel a kind, safe and welcoming environment.
I am currently working with an Employment Consultant, she has been amazing to be working with.

Woodgreen is very supportive to newcomers. They assisted me with my resume and finding a job. Thanks to the nature of their regular communications, which helped me a lot. My special thanks to Eden Abraham for her kind support. I would highly recommend Woodgreen to newcomers.

So much for helping the community.  Citizens get nothing.  Unbelievable what's happening to our "community"

address: 1090 Highbury Avenue, London, ON N5Y 4V9
